text: "NTA - a last minute trip to the beach, with a toddler, when you’ve already planned things and have been doing all the organising - while 5 months pregnant? text: "ESH. If your husband asked to go the beach earlier, would you have said yes? Why did you ask him to get up with the toddler if he could sleep in? He could have provided more ideas. text: "ESH - he can take some initiative but if he did the same on mother's day you'd probably be a little irked too"

text: "Nta. No way in hell I'd be okay with a last minute beach trip when I tried to plan to accommodate him. Granted my husband would hop to action if he really wanted to get. But my urs sounds lazy. No. I'm pregnant and have a toddler. He gets dinner."

text: "NTA for NO to the last minute beach trip. I live in a beach tourist town with a toddler. The prep time plus drive time is 3 plus hours if we dont have to stop for food gas and sun screen plus parking on father's day weekend that's not an on the fly kinda trip."

text: "NTA - Father’s Day isn’t Father’s Day Off, he should still be helping with the kids. You can’t be expected to juggle everything, especially at 5 months pregnant. label: NTA

text: "NTA. Most men don’t realize the mental load it takes to plan things related to housework and family because they are used to their wives doing it for them. He’s also a child for emotionally punishing you the rest of the day instead of just communicating like a fucking adult."

text: "NTA. He said all he wanted was for OP to make his favorite meal. She repeatedly checked in with him to make sure that that was what he wanted. She went to a lot of effort to get ready for the thing he repeatedly asked for, only for him to pull the rug out from under her that morning, and make last minute plans. Husband should have communicated better or kept his mouth shut. I'd feel that way if genders were reversed too. Communication goes both ways."
label: NTA

text: "YTA first off how do you expect him to sleep in when you also expect him to get up and change the diaper? You should say what you meant, which is he didn’t get to sleep in because you wanted him to get up and change a diaper. Your attitude in general seems very “my way or nothing”."

text: "YTA. You didn't do anything for him without giving him extra tasks and complaints. You didn't celebrate him, you were just marking off Father's Day. Unless this is the same energy he gave towards Mother's Day, YTA."

text: "YTA - I can only imagine how annoying this must have been for him. I’m confused because it honestly seems like you perhaps thought that your post would make those of us reading it consider you to be a victim here. Why? In all honesty, you come across quite entitled and unpleasant to be around. Instead of being a partner willing to go the extra mile for him on Father’s Day , you sure spent a lot of time in your post putting him down and making it about how unfair things are for you: I could keep going on, but I’m actually starting to get annoyed. So again, YTA. For future, follow Master Yoda’s wisdom “do or do not, there is no try” (and I don’t think you even tried). I hope that things go better next year."
label: YTA

text: "Either way ESH because it sounds like you were all prepared to give him what he wanted but reacted poorly to his last minute suggestion AND he waited too long to tell you what he wanted and expected you to drop everything to give it to him with zero effort on his part."
label: ESH
